summary: A biographical overview of Walt Disney, detailing his pioneering work in the American animation industry, the creation of Mickey Mouse, his push for feature-length animated films, and his eventual expansion into television and theme parks like Disneyland and the conceptual EPCOT.

principles.statement: Animation must be driven by emotionally gripping stories.
principles.rationale: He realized that technical animation alone was not enough to hold an audience's interest; they needed a compelling plot, which led him to create a dedicated story department separate from the animators.

principles.statement: Amusement parks should be clean, unspoiled places where both children and parents can have fun together.
principles.rationale: He was dissatisfied with the condition of the parks he visited with his daughters and wanted to create a shared, family-friendly environment.

principles.statement: A community of tomorrow must never be completed.
principles.rationale: He believed in constant innovation, where new materials, systems, and ideas are perpetually introduced, tested, and demonstrated to showcase ingenuity.
principles.source_quote: It will be a community of tomorrow that will never be completed, but will always be introducing and testing and demonstrating new materials and systems.

mental_models.name: The Public Persona vs. Private Self
mental_models.description: Separating the idealized brand identity from the flawed human reality. Disney recognized that the 'Walt Disney' the public knew was a pristine character that didn't share his personal vices.
mental_models.example: He acknowledged the facade to a friend, stating that the public 'Walt Disney' does not smoke or drink, whereas he privately did both.

heuristics: Design characters to be 'peppy, alert, saucy and venturesome, keeping him also neat and trim.'
Use 'That'll work' as the ultimate indicator of high praise to maintain exceptionally high expectations among staff.

signature_quotes.text: It will be a community of tomorrow that will never be completed, but will always be introducing and testing and demonstrating new materials and systems. And EPCOT will always be a showcase to the world for the ingenuity and imagination of American free enterprise.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his vision for the Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow (EPCOT).

signature_quotes.text: I'm not Walt Disney. I do a lot of things Walt Disney would not do. Walt Disney does not smoke. I smoke. Walt Disney does not drink. I drink.
signature_quotes.context: Acknowledging the difference between his private self and his carefully cultivated public persona.

anti_patterns.description: Creating entertainment spaces exclusively for children.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It excludes parents from the experience. Disney envisioned and built Disneyland specifically so that both children and their parents could have fun together in a clean environment.

anti_patterns.description: Treating a city or community project as a finished product.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It prevents the integration of emerging technologies. Disney warned that a true community of tomorrow must 'never be completed' so it can constantly test new ideas.
